summary: I am currently a sophomore attending the University of Minnesota Twin Cities. Being a Hmong American student here, I balance my cultural traditions with my academic life. The university has been a place where I continue to grow and explore my interests beyond high school. It feels like a second home as I pursue my education and personal development.

Every year, I celebrate the Hmong New Year with my community, wearing traditional clothing and enjoying great food. We spend the day at the River Centre, which is a central place for our festivities. This event is very important to me as it connects me to my heritage and allows me to see many of my peers and family members. It’s a time of joy and cultural pride.

The RiverCentre is where we gather every year to celebrate the Hmong New Year. It’s a special place for me because it hosts so many cultural events that bring our community together. I look forward to spending the day there, surrounded by family and friends, enjoying the festivities. It really feels like the heart of our celebration.

I was actually born in Phanat Nikhom, Thailand, in a refugee camp. There’s a photo of me at just 20 days old with my mom there. It’s a place that marks the beginning of my life before my family moved to the United States a year later. Even though I grew up here, Phanat Nikhom is an important part of my story and identity.

Thailand is where I was born, specifically in a refugee camp in Phanat Nikhom. My early days were spent there before my family immigrated to the United States when I was about a year old. Though I don’t remember much from that time, it’s a significant part of my background and my family’s journey. Thailand represents the start of our new life before coming to America.

My family came to the United States when I was about a year old, and this is where I’ve grown up and built my life. Being a Hmong American here has shaped much of who I am, from celebrating cultural events like the Hmong New Year to attending school and university. The United States has given me opportunities to explore my talents in music and sports, and it’s where I continue to pursue my dreams.
